result.ui_display: 이런... Duck.ai를 일시적으로 사용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 시도하세요.
console_errors: An iframe which has both allow-scripts and allow-same-origin for its sandbox attribute can escape its sandboxing.

analysis.error_change: Previous tests showed ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR or ERR_RESPONSE_PARSING. This test shows ERR_HTTP2_COMPRESSION_ERROR — a NEW error type.
analysis.compression_error_meaning: ERR_HTTP2_COMPRESSION_ERROR indicates HPACK header compression corruption. The Etap proxy generates HTTP/2 response headers with invalid HPACK encoding when injecting SSE responses.
analysis.reload_templates_confirmed: reload_templates WAS effective — the error changed from previous patterns, proving the new template IS being used.
analysis.sse_vs_json_comparison: #404 JSON template → 200 OK, no errors. #405 SSE template → ERR_HTTP2_COMPRESSION_ERROR. SSE content-type (text/event-stream) in response headers causes H2 HPACK corruption.
analysis.root_cause: Etap proxy writes raw HTTP/1.1-style headers into H2 frames. For JSON (application/json), this accidentally works. For SSE (text/event-stream + chunked transfer), the header encoding breaks H2 HPACK state.
analysis.conclusion: SSE injection through H2 proxy is fundamentally broken at the transport layer. JSON templates are the only viable path. Warning display must use a different mechanism than SSE streaming.

comparison_with_previous.vs_404_json: #404 duckduckgo_json → 200 OK, no H2 errors, JSON body received. #405 SSE → complete connection failure.
comparison_with_previous.vs_previous_sse_tests: All previous SSE tests (#389, #397, #400) had reload_templates NOT applied. #405 is the FIRST real SSE test. Result: even worse — COMPRESSION_ERROR not just PROTOCOL_ERROR.
comparison_with_previous.pattern: JSON templates = transport works. SSE templates = transport completely broken.

recommendation: Abandon SSE injection approach entirely. Use JSON-only templates for all services. Develop alternative warning delivery: (1) inject warning via page-level script, (2) use block page redirect, or (3) modify frontend expectations.
